metric_value: To be Y2K-safe by 1/1/2000: Y2K-safe applications in place by 9/30/98 for 5 quarters of testing/cutover; 12-month renovation/replacement program started by 9/30/97.
Customers, suppliers, and competitors may be in trouble; existing value chains have every probability of experiencing broken links; opportunity to move into competitors' territory if they fail.
Must audit Y2K progress of customers and suppliers and test EDI links through 1999.
Renovate / Replace / Outsource / Retire — four options for each Y2K-affected application.
Majority of US enterprises are acting as though they have 2-3 years to fix Y2K, but true deadline is 15 months earlier; 'if you are not working on the problem today, you may have already failed'.

No catastrophic Y2K failures on 1/1/2000 attributable to major enterprises that followed the recommended remediation timeline; enterprises that started late (post-1998) reported higher cost overruns and near-misses.
EDI links were a frequent source of Y2K remediation scope creep in 1999; Kastner's early-audit recommendation was widely adopted through 1998-1999.
